index.name: Nifty Pharma Index
index.description: Nifty Pharma tracks 20 companies in India — world's 3rd largest pharma market by volume, 14th by value. India's title 'pharmacy of the world': 20% of global generic medicines, 60% of global vaccines. Structural advantages: 60-70% cost discount vs US/Europe enables aggressive pricing for emerging markets and tender wins. Two profit pools: (1) US generics (40% revenue for Sun Pharma, Dr Reddy's, Aurobindo) — each USD generics approval adds ~₹50-100 Cr annual profit; (2) Domestic branded generics (30% revenue) — 50%+ margins, resists price cuts, 15-20% growth. Exports headwind: generic pricing pressure from US competitors and China (world's largest API manufacturer, undercutting costs). Regulatory advantage: Indian companies' FDA-approved facilities number 200+ globally, creating moat vs competitors from other emerging markets. Capex efficiency: INR 1 Cr capex generates $10-15 Cr annual revenue vs global pharma ratio of $1-3 Cr. Volatility drivers: US FDA approvals (binary events creating 20-50% stock moves), complex generics timelines (4-6 years to approval vs simple generics 2 years). Long-term: biosimilars provide 15-20% premiums vs simple generics but require $200-300M capex and regulatory expertise concentrated at Tier-1 companies.
